sourceMovie.title: Little Miss Sunshine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Little Miss Sunshine (2006) as a comedy theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The comedy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Edge of Seventeen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Little Miss Sunshine clicked for you, The Edge of Seventeen (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in comedy and drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Little Miss Sunshine?
faq.answer: Try The Edge of Seventeen, Kodachrome, Jamon Jamon—each shares DNA with Little Miss Sunshine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Little Miss Sunshine?
faq.answer: Little Miss Sunshine (2006) is best described as Comedy, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Little Miss Sunshine worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.7/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this comedy lane.
